pi_items.source: Live environment test confirmed file exists at this path (15029 bytes, owned by ga:ga). Setup script creates it via python-pptx -> libreoffice --headless --convert-to odp, then moves to this path.

pi_items.metadata_value: ["Web App", "Mobile App", "API Gateway", "User Service", "Product Service", "Order Service", "Payment Service", "PostgreSQL", "MongoDB", "Redis Cache"]
pi_items.verified_value: ["Web App", "Mobile App", "API Gateway", "User Service", "Product Service", "Order Service", "Payment Service", "PostgreSQL", "MongoDB", "Redis Cache"]
pi_items.source: All 10 labels are explicitly named in the task description. Counted: 2 client boxes (Web App, Mobile App) + 1 gateway (API Gateway) + 4 services (User Service, Product Service, Order Service, Payment Service) + 3 data stores (PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis Cache) = 10 total. Each label is quoted verbatim in the description.
Task description explicitly names all three tiers: 'Client Tier (top of slide)', 'Service Tier (middle)', 'Data Tier (bottom)'. Description also says 'labeled with the tier name (e.g. Client Tier)'.

The task description specifies exactly 10 distinct component boxes: 2 client + 1 gateway + 4 services + 3 data stores = 10. The metadata value of 9 is a lenient threshold (allows 1 miss), not the exact expected count. The true expected count from the description is 10.

Counted from task description: 'both client boxes to API Gateway' (2) + 'API Gateway to each of the four services' (4) + 'User Service to PostgreSQL' (1) + 'Product Service to PostgreSQL' (1) + 'Order Service to both PostgreSQL and MongoDB' (2) + 'Payment Service to PostgreSQL' (1) = 11 total. Matches the 11 pairs in the connections list exactly.
Task description says 'Enclose each tier in a large dashed-border rectangle'. There are 3 tiers (Client, Service, Data), so 3 enclosures required. Matches layer_names count.

pi_items.verified_value: 11 directed pairs: Web App->API Gateway, Mobile App->API Gateway, API Gateway->User Service, API Gateway->Product Service, API Gateway->Order Service, API Gateway->Payment Service, User Service->PostgreSQL, Product Service->PostgreSQL, Order Service->PostgreSQL, Order Service->MongoDB, Payment Service->PostgreSQL
pi_items.source: Each connection traced to specific phrases in the task description. All 11 are explicitly stated. Cross-verified: connections form a valid 3-tier DAG with API Gateway as the central hub. PostgreSQL has 4 incoming connections (from all 4 services), MongoDB has 1 (from Order Service), Redis Cache has 0 (not connected per description).

pi_items.source: Live environment ODP XML parse confirmed: slide count = 2, text content = ['E-Commerce Platform Overview', 'Architecture & Design Document', 'System Architecture']. Screenshots evidence_03 and evidence_05 visually confirm both slides.

privileged_info_summary: The agent must create a microservices architecture diagram on Slide 2 of a 2-slide ODP presentation. The starter file has Slide 1 titled 'E-Commerce Platform Overview' and Slide 2 titled 'System Architecture' (blank canvas, widescreen 33.87cm x 19.05cm). The completed diagram must contain exactly 10 labeled component boxes organized in 3 horizontal tiers: Client Tier at top with 'Web App' and 'Mobile App' (orange fill), Service Tier in middle with 'API Gateway' (dark blue fill) connected down to 'User Service', 'Product Service', 'Order Service', 'Payment Service' (blue fill), and Data Tier at bottom with 'PostgreSQL', 'MongoDB' (green fill) and 'Redis Cache' (red fill). There must be exactly 11 connector arrows: 2 from clients to API Gateway, 4 from API Gateway to each service, and 5 from services to data stores (User->PostgreSQL, Product->PostgreSQL, Order->PostgreSQL, Order->MongoDB, Payment->PostgreSQL). Note: Redis Cache has NO incoming connections in the specification. Each tier must be enclosed in a dashed-border rectangle with no fill, labeled with the tier name. All component boxes must have centered white bold text. The file must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/Presentations/platform_architecture.odp. Key data quirk: the connector tool in LibreOffice Impress creates draw:connector XML elements (not draw:line), and the task description says 'connector arrows' specifically.
